claim: The intelligence-serving layer needs its own internal compatibility API before deeper internal rewiring or file moves.
mechanism: Serving is the broadest surface in the repo because it spans runtime loading, context injection, advisory logic, and MCP delivery. Introducing a single serving namespace gives the CLI and other top-level callers one stable seam while leaving deeper module relationships untouched. That lowers the risk of circular-import accidents during later structural work.
boundary: This namespace does not yet normalize all internal serving dependencies. It only creates the first compatibility layer for top-level callers.
